paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the Erdős-Hajnal property
summary: Conjecture 1.8 (the 'polynomial Nikiforov' conjecture) asserts that every graph H is viral, i.e., satisfies a polynomial-bound version of Nikiforov's theorem via ε-restricted subsets. Bucić, Fox, and Pham (arXiv:2403.08303, 2024) proved that the Erdős-Hajnal property is equivalent to being viral, establishing that Conjecture 1.8 is in turn equivalent to the full Erdős-Hajnal conjecture; since the latter remains open, so does Conjecture 1.8. No direct proof or counterexample for Conjecture 1.8 was found in the 2025–2026 literature.

since_posted.title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures
since_posted.authors: Matija Bucić, Jacob Fox, Huy Tuan Pham
since_posted.claim: Proves that having the Erdős-Hajnal property is equivalent to being viral (i.e., satisfying Conjecture 1.8), thereby reducing the conjecture to the full Erdős-Hajnal conjecture.

search_queries: Nguyen Scott Seymour "induced subgraph density" "viral" Erdős-Hajnal 2307.06455
notes: Conjecture 1.8 is equivalent to the full Erdős-Hajnal conjecture via the Bucić-Fox-Pham equivalence (arXiv:2403.08303). The source paper was first posted in July 2023; the 2026-04-18 date is the journal publication, which already cites Bucić-Fox-Pham as reference [4]. No further resolution found in the literature.
